definition: The net-new, consumer side of a providerŌåöconsumer seam-contract: the consumer declares the subset of a protocol's dimensions/capabilities it actually depends on (consumerRequires), rather than the whole protocol. The provider side is reused from the existing protocol conformance contract (the provider declares its tier). Compatible Ōćö the consumer's required subset is contained in what the provider's tier supplies; a drift is any change removing a required member.
usage: Part 2 of the ProviderŌĆōConsumer Graph Protocol (#401, #092 Fork 2). The only new schema at the seam ŌĆö it deliberately reuses protocols.json rather than coining a parallel Pact-style format. Feeds drift detection, impact analysis, and continuous verification on one definition.
